must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions 'exposure in Python' under programming languages — no projects, no depth demonstrated.

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This is an automotive system test engineer with 4 years of ECU/ADAS/infotainment validation experience, not a Python backend developer. His Python exposure is a single parenthetical mention ('exposure in Python') buried in a skills list — there are zero Python projects, zero backend frameworks (Django/Flask/FastAPI), zero database experience, and zero web technology skills. The recruiter explicitly wants someone with Python projects and AI model training experience; this candidate has neither. His actual expertise is in CAPL scripting, CANoe, UDS protocols, HIL testing, and AUTOSAR — none of which transfer to this role. Do not shortlist.
strengths: AUTOMOTIVE TESTING DEPTH: 4 years of hands-on ECU validation across Infotainment, ADAS, and Telematics systems for clients like General Motors and Skoda — strong domain expertise, but entirely irrelevant to a Python backend role.

TEST AUTOMATION BACKGROUND: Developed and executed complex test cases using CAPL scripting in CANoe, reducing manual testing time — demonstrates automation thinking, but in a completely different language and toolchain than required.

INDUSTRY STANDARDS COMPLIANCE: Practical experience with ASPICE, ISO 26262, and V&V processes — shows structured engineering discipline, though not applicable to the Python dev or AI training requirements of this role.
gaps: NO PYTHON BACKEND DEVELOPMENT: The recruiter requires Python projects and AI model training experience. This candidate lists only 'exposure in Python' with zero demonstrated projects, no frameworks (Django/Flask/FastAPI), and no AI/ML work — this is a complete miss on the core requirement.

WRONG DOMAIN AND TOOLCHAIN: The candidate's entire 4-year career is automotive embedded systems testing (CANoe, CAPL, CAN protocols, HIL benches) — there is no overlap with web technologies, databases, REST APIs, Docker, or any skill on the JD's required list.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated and JD has no maximum specified — no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office in Bangalore; candidate is based in Chennai (Jasmin Infotech, Chennai 600100).
JD requires 0 relevant experience — no penalty applies regardless of candidate background.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in JD — no penalty applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Jasmin Infotech is listed as ongoing ('March 2022 to present') with no notice period stated.
alternative_role_fit: Automotive System Test Engineer / ADAS Validation Engineer
rare_assets: HIL bench testing expertise with Dyna4 and VT-Studio for ADAS ECU validation — rare specialisation in automotive embedded testing.
UDS protocol depth with hands-on service ID-level diagnostics (10, 11, 22, 2E, 3E, 27, 31, 19, 28, 85, 14) — highly niche automotive knowledge.
CAPL scripting for automated test scenarios in CANoe across Infotainment, ADAS, and cluster systems — a rare automotive test automation skill.
self_reported_unverified: 'Significantly reducing manual testing time' via CAPL scripting — no quantified baseline or percentage improvement provided.
'Led defect tracing efforts' on ADAS systems — no team size or scope of leadership mentioned to verify seniority of this claim.
'Improving system stability and overall ADAS performance' — outcome claim with no metrics or before/after data to substantiate.
interview_flags: Probe actual depth of Python — 'exposure in Python' is vague; ask for any code written, scripts developed, or GitHub profile.
Ask whether candidate has any interest or background in AI/ML model training, as recruiter specifically requires this.
Clarify willingness to relocate from Chennai to Bangalore for an in-office role.
Probe whether candidate is open to a complete domain switch from automotive embedded testing to Python backend development.
Ask about compensation expectations — current role is 4 years of mid-level automotive work, and the JD budget (4-6 LPA) may be a significant downgrade.
